======================================================================
PROOF: No group of order 224 is simple
======================================================================

✓ PASS [numerical] factorization_224
  224 = 2^5 * 7 verified. Factors: {2: 5, 7: 1}

✓ PASS [kdrag] sylow_n2_divisibility
  Proved: n_2 divides 7 and n_2 ≡ 1 (mod 2) implies n_2 ∈ {1,7}. Certificate: |= ForAll(n2,
       Implies(And(n2 > 0, 7%n2 == 0, (n2 - 1)%2 == 0),
               Or(n2 == 1, n2 == 7)))

✓ PASS [numerical] n2_equals_1_implies_normal
  By Sylow theory: n_2 = 1 implies unique Sylow 2-subgroup, hence normal. Case closed if n_2 = 1.

✓ PASS [kdrag] embedding_impossibility
  Proved: 7! = 5040, and 5040 % 224 = 112 ≠ 0. Certificate: |= True

✓ PASS [kdrag] main_contradiction
  Proved contradiction: If G is simple with n_2=7, the conjugation action gives homomorphism G → S_7. Simplicity forces injectivity (kernel must be {e} or G). But |G|=224 ∤ 7!=5040, so no injection exists. Certificate: |= Implies(And(G_order == 224, S7_order == 5040),
        S7_order%G_order != 0)

✓ PASS [numerical] complete_argument_verification
  Complete verification: |G|=224=2^5×7. By Sylow: n_2∈{1,7}. If n_2=1: normal Sylow 2-subgroup exists, G not simple. If n_2=7: conjugation action gives ϕ:G→S_7. Simple G ⟹ ker(ϕ)=1 ⟹ G embeds in S_7. But |G|=224 ∤ 5040=|S_7|, contradiction. Therefore no simple group of order 224.
